title: Real Electronics — Build Your First Project
subtitle: LED Circuits, Arduino UNO & Simulation Tools
overview: From circuit theory to your first working project! We go from paper diagrams to real breadboard circuits. Understand how current flows, meet Arduino UNO, and learn to simulate before you build.

slides._note: Export slides from 'Section2_Electronics.pptx' as JPEG/PNG into sessions/session-3/slides/
slides._pdf_note: Alternatively place your exported PDF here. The viewer supports both image slides and PDF.

keyTopics: LED Circuit — Battery + Resistor + LED complete loop
How Current Flows in a Complete Loop (open = stops)
Arduino UNO — 14 Digital I/O, 6 Analog, USB, 5V
Microcontroller vs Microprocessor — What's the difference?
Why Engineers Use Arduino — open-source, affordable
Simulation Tools: Arduino IDE, Wokwi, Tinkercad
Simulate Before You Build — Industry Best Practice
Engineering Process: Idea → Simulate → Build → Present

activity.title: Simulate then Build — LED Blink with Arduino
steps: Step 1: Go to wokwi.com and open Arduino UNO template
Step 2: Add LED and resistor in the simulation
Step 3: Write the blink code (void setup / void loop)
Step 4: Run simulation — see LED blink!
Step 5: Now build the real circuit on breadboard
Step 6: Upload code to real Arduino and watch it blink!

quiz.q: In a complete LED circuit, what happens when you disconnect one wire?
options: The circuit loop breaks — LED goes off
quiz.explanation: Electricity needs a COMPLETE LOOP to flow. Break the loop (disconnect any wire) → current stops → LED off.

quiz.q: Why is a resistor ESSENTIAL in an LED circuit?
options: To make the LED glow different colours
To limit current and protect the LED from burning out
To make the circuit look more complex
quiz.explanation: Without a resistor, too much current flows through the LED and destroys it instantly. The resistor is the LED's bodyguard!

quiz.q: How many Digital I/O pins does Arduino UNO have?
quiz.explanation: Arduino UNO has 14 Digital I/O pins! These can be INPUT (read sensor) or OUTPUT (control LED, motor).

quiz.q: What operating voltage does Arduino UNO use?
quiz.explanation: Arduino UNO operates at 5V — safe for beginners! This is the voltage on digital pins when set to HIGH.

quiz.q: What is the main difference between Microcontroller and Microprocessor?
options: Microcontroller has CPU+Memory+I/O all in one chip; Microprocessor only has CPU
They are exactly the same thing
quiz.explanation: Microcontroller = All-in-one (CPU + Memory + I/O). Microprocessor = Just the CPU. That's why Arduino is a microcontroller!

quiz.q: Which chip is the Microcontroller inside Arduino UNO?
quiz.explanation: ATmega328P is the microcontroller chip inside Arduino UNO! It has everything needed to run programs and control hardware.

options: A free online Arduino circuit simulator
quiz.explanation: Wokwi (wokwi.com) is a FREE browser-based Arduino simulator. Write code and test circuits virtually — no hardware needed!

quiz.q: What is Tinkercad used for in electronics?
options: A visual drag-and-drop circuit builder with code simulation
quiz.explanation: Tinkercad Circuits lets you drag-and-drop components AND write code to simulate. Perfect for beginners!

quiz.q: Why do professional engineers simulate before building?
options: Because they don't have real components
To save time and money — mistakes in simulation are free
quiz.explanation: Simulate first = No component damage, zero cost for mistakes, easy debugging. Standard industry practice!

quiz.q: What is the correct engineering process order?
quiz.explanation: 01 IDEA → 02 SIMULATE → 03 BUILD → 04 PRESENT. Always simulate before touching real components!

quiz.q: What does Arduino IDE stand for?
quiz.explanation: IDE = Integrated Development Environment! Software where you write, compile, and upload Arduino code.

quiz.q: How do you upload code to an Arduino?
options: Via USB connection from a computer
quiz.explanation: Connect Arduino to computer via USB, write code in Arduino IDE, click upload. Code sent through USB cable!

quiz.q: What does the LED convert electrical energy into?
options: Light (and a tiny amount of heat)
quiz.explanation: LED = Light Emitting Diode! Converts electrical energy into LIGHT. Very efficient at doing it!

quiz.q: What does 'open source' mean for Arduino?
options: The box is open when you buy it
The design and software are free for anyone to use, modify, and share
quiz.explanation: Arduino is open-source! Hardware designs and software are free — anyone can use, modify, and share them.

quiz.q: Before building a real circuit, what should you do FIRST?
quiz.explanation: Always SIMULATE first! Test digitally before building. Mistakes cost nothing in simulation!
